mechanism_narrative: Sustained low unemployment indicates a strong labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns. A healthy job market reduces economic uncertainty and encourages investment.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative on average during periods where UNRATE is below its 10-year moving average over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is cut significantly, it signals aggressive monetary easing to stimulate the economy, which typically boosts equity valuations by lowering the cost of capital and increasing liquidity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If, following a trigger, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales and declining unemployment indicates robust consumer demand and a healthy job market, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 forward returns positively over the short term.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return is not statistically significant (p-value > 0.1) during triggered periods over any 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a sustained decline in industrial production signals weakening economic activity and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are revised downwards.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive on average during triggered periods within an identified contractionary regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts reflects strong investor and consumer confidence, indicating future economic activity and demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a very short horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between HOUST growth and S&P 500 5-day forward returns is consistently negative or zero (Rank IC < 0.01) over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in non-farm payrolls indicates robust job creation and economic expansion, boosting consumer confidence and corporate revenues, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: If, after a trigger, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with rising interest rates signals a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing overheating demand, which typically leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive on average during periods where both CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 4% and FEDFUNDS is above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, a significant rebound in GDP growth signals a strong economic turnaround, which improves corporate earnings outlooks and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average during triggered periods within an identified post-recession recovery regime.
